name: Hypothermia
summary: Core body temperature dropping. Risk in cold, wet, windy conditions. Children lose heat faster than adults.
recognition: Progressing: confusion, mumbling, fumbling, stumbling (the three M's)

callEmergency.text: Call 999 if you can't warm them, they're confused, or they lose consciousness.

steps: Keep them moving — get to shelter.
Warm them gradually with clothing, blankets or towels (warmed if possible).

warnings.text: Children lose heat faster than adults — higher surface area to mass ratio, less insulating fat.
Don't use direct strong heat (hot water, fires) — warm gradually.
